pages.full_text:
Saturday June 20th.
4 of the 5 boys are moving house this summer. This week John is holidaying in L.A. William has just moved into the farm. Charles is storing stuff in our garage before moving into a room while doing up his new house. Peter moves to London next week. John moves [from]

[Aerial photograph of a triangular building with surrounding grounds]

[Rotated caption beside photo:] Rushton Triangle Lodge 1593. Built by [?Tresham] RC. after being freed from prison.
photo_captions: Rushton Triangle Lodge 1593. Built by [?Tresham] RC. after being freed from prison.
pages.notes: The year 2015 is inferred from context: the left page entry mentions 'John is holidaying in L.A.' and 'John moves from Swansea to Cardiff' (text continuing on right page), which closely matches the B_0247 entry (April 10th 2015) where 'John in Swansea about to move to Cardiff.' June 20, 2015 was indeed a Saturday, supporting the year identification. The text at the bottom right of the left page is cut off at the gutter; it continues on the right page with 'Swansea to Cardiff.' The caption beside the photo is written vertically/rotated. The author name in the caption is partially legible — 'Tresham' is the most likely reading (Sir Thomas Tresham built Rushton Triangular Lodge); 'RC' = Roman Catholic. No handwritten page number visible in the top-left corner.

pages.full_text:
Swansea to Cardiff.
        At. 10.30. Simon, Eve Charles & Tinder left for Naseby. It's a good viewpoint.
Prince [?Charles] [strikethrough: Rupert] cavalry chased the New Army cavalry away & the Cromwell [?'s] infantry killed the Cavalier infantry.
        Then on to the Triangular Lodge.
A most odd, small building built by RC. He was the grandfather of a gunpowder plotter, married to a Throckmorton.
        I had had a sore throat for 2 nights before the trip + suddenly at Naseby it became a cold. When we got to Top End*** campsite at Little Staughton, I stayed inside while Simon + Charles sat outside + walked. Half the site is given over to a Ford Corsair rally. Very serious business. Simon + Charles are spending much time discussing Morgan engineering.
        We had a quiet night.
pages.notes: The handwritten page number in the top-right corner is only partially visible and appears to show '1' but is cut off — could be '71', '75' or another two-digit number; reported as null since it cannot be read with confidence. The strikethrough over 'Rupert' (replacing with 'Charles') is a correction in the text — the writer first wrote 'Prince Rupert' then crossed it out and wrote 'Charles' above, though in context 'Rupert' is historically correct for Naseby (Prince Rupert commanded the Royalist cavalry). The '***' after 'Top End' appears to be annotation marks (stars/asterisks). 'RC' in 'built by RC' refers to Sir Thomas Tresham (Roman Catholic), consistent with the photo caption on the left page. 'Throckmorton' — Francis Tresham's mother was Muriel Throckmorton. 'Ford Corsair rally' — unusual car club meeting at the campsite. 'Morgan engineering' — likely referring to Morgan motor cars.
